message:
fix(tui): Ensure truncation uses visual width for unicode CJK/emoji layout safety

Terminal UI elements calculating padding and truncation were previously using `.chars().count()` (codepoints) or `.len()` (bytes) instead of `.width()` (visual columns). When handling multi-width characters like CJK or Emoji, this caused string slices to exceed the physical terminal bounds, breaking the layout and crashing the alternate screen buffer with unhandled scroll events.

Migrated all TUI truncation and padding logic to strictly use `unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
